steps.title: Identify the key principle
steps.content: A single random sample gives an estimate of a population parameter, not a guaranteed value. Sampling variability means another sample will generally produce a different proportion, and results from one city say nothing about another city.

steps.title: Evaluate each Roman numeral statement
steps.content:
Statement I says the entire city must be 78% satisfied — but 78% is a sample estimate, not proof. Not necessarily true.
Statement II says another random sample of 1,000 adults must also yield 78% — sampling variability rules this out. Not necessarily true.
Statement III says a sample from a different city must yield 78% — a different population can have any satisfaction rate. Not necessarily true.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• A) None: matches our finding that no statement must be true. Keep.
  • B) II only: II is not guaranteed. Eliminate.
  • C) I and II only: neither is guaranteed. Eliminate.
  • D) I and III only: neither is guaranteed. Eliminate.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
